Two 7 x 6 inch NaI(Tl) detectors for measuring
independence of the capture gamma-ray cascade.
.New method for determining p-wave assignment of U-238
resonances presented consisting of measuring the
fraction of capture gamma-rays above 4.3 MeV.
In this rel.ref. some of resonances assigned as p-wave

Re-normalization factor was applied to the data such

in the range 100-1700 eV are in agreement with the

It was assumed that first group contains only s-wave
resonances, while the 2nd group contains mostly p-wave
but also a small number of s-wave resonances.
Porter-Thomas distribution was used for fitting.
63 resonances with g*Gn1 > 2 meV were used.
Data from this experiment for Gn< 10 meV and data of
G.Carraro+ for Gn> 10 meV were used in analysis.
